Approach Summary: Use a hash map. For each number iterate through the array and check if its complement (target - num) already exists in the map. If yes return indices. If no store current number and index.
Key Insight: The complement trick eliminates the need for a nested loop. Store what you need to find, not what you've seen.

Approach Summary: Expand Around Center approach. For each character (and each gap between characters) expand outward while characters match. Track the longest found.
Key Insight: Two cases: odd-length (single center) and even-length (two-char center). Both must be handled separately.

Approach Summary: Combine a doubly linked list with a hash map. Hash map gives O(1) access to nodes. Linked list maintains access order — most recent at head, least recent at tail.
Key Insight: The dummy head and tail nodes simplify edge case handling enormously. Always add new nodes right after head.

Approach Summary: BFS using a queue. Push root then process level by level. Track level size before processing so you can group nodes per level correctly.
Key Insight: Key is capturing queue.length at the start of each outer loop iteration to know how many nodes belong to the current level.

Problem Name: Find Minimum in Rotated Sorted Array
Approach Summary: Modified binary search. Compare mid with right pointer. If mid > right the minimum is in the right half. Otherwise it's in the left half (inclusive of mid).
Key Insight: Don't compare with left pointer — comparing with right is the key insight. The right side always tells you which half is unsorted.
